source_map.role: provider adapter, instance registry, durable session binding, canonical runtime event, and recovery boundary
generated ACP schema and symmetric client/agent peer transport
typed orchestration, worktree, checkpoint, command, event, and projection contracts
scoped client projections, resumable streams, keyed command scheduling, and environment authorization
browser-held DPoP key and per-request proof creation
request verification and atomic replay-key consumption

architecture_map: provider intent -> schema validation -> provider-instance adapter -> durable thread binding -> canonical runtime event -> independent subscribers
typed snapshot/cache -> exact sequence cursor -> resumable event stream -> stale-safe pure reducer -> scoped projection -> fail-soft persistence
thread workspace metadata -> explicit worktree selection -> cwd-keyed command serialization -> terminal/diff/tool routing
one generated ACP schema -> inverse client/agent RPC groups -> one scoped duplex NDJSON transport -> typed core and extension dispatch
persistent P-256 browser key -> method/URL/token-bound proof -> strict verification -> atomic thumbprint+jti replay consumption

glossary.meaning: stable enabled runtime identity distinct from a provider driver kind
durable thread-to-provider-instance routing and resume record
monotonic sequence represented by a snapshot and used to resume event delivery
structural ACP role: server for one RPC group and client for the inverse group
digest identity of the public P-256 proof key

patterns.mechanism: A typed provider facade routes by explicit provider-instance identity, persists thread bindings and resume metadata, rejects adapter/event identity mismatches, and republishes canonical runtime events to independent consumers.
preconditions: provider resume cursor where recovery is required
traps: driver kind is not sufficient routing identity
missing binding or resume cursor prevents recovery
provider-neutrality fails if provider-only facts are erased
approval and user-input callbacks are not shown as restart-durable by this selected corpus
portable_playbook: define a typed adapter and capabilities
persist stable provider-instance bindings
validate source identity on every event
separate canonical event consumers
test at least two heterogeneous adapters plus restart and mismatch rejection

patterns.mechanism: Typed snapshots and events seed scoped Effect state, resume from exact sequence cursors, pass through stale-safe pure reducers, persist the newest projection on bounded fail-soft lanes, and expose environment/thread-keyed views.
preconditions: exact base snapshot or socket fallback
traps: an event before any base snapshot is dropped
live state can be newer than debounced fail-soft persistence
unknown events are no-ops and may not advance a cursor
registry-local scheduling is not a cross-process lock
portable_playbook: type snapshot/event/resume contracts
use a pure replay-idempotent reducer
seed then subscribe after the exact cursor
scope keyed projections
test replay, reconnect, deletion, and persistence failure

patterns.mechanism: Branch and worktree are typed thread metadata; the UI requires an explicit base for new-worktree mode, every work surface resolves the thread worktree before the project root, and VCS mutations serialize per environment and cwd so isolated worktrees may progress independently.
preconditions: cwd propagation to every tool surface
traps: parallel means isolated cwd lanes, not concurrent mutation in one checkout
absent base branch fails closed
stale shell metadata can misroute tools
selected client corpus does not prove server-side worktree cleanup
portable_playbook: persist workspace identity on the thread
require an explicit base
publish the authoritative path before execution
route VCS, terminal, diff, and tools by that path
serialize per workspace key and surface isolation visibly

patterns.mechanism: One generated ACP schema forms two inverse RPC groups: the client serves client-side requests while calling agent RPCs, and the agent serves agent RPCs while calling client-side requests, both over one scoped duplex transport with explicit core-method classification and disjoint request-ID spaces.
traps: wrong core-method classification misroutes requests
pending extension requests must be drained on interruption and termination
handler isolation can hide subscriber failure unless separately observed
raw protocol logging may disclose wire content
portable_playbook: derive inverse RPC groups from one schema
reserve disjoint ID spaces
classify core methods explicitly
negotiate then enforce capabilities
fail pending requests on exit and test malformed frames, zero IDs, and extension collision

patterns.mechanism: A persistent browser P-256 key signs ES256 proofs bound to method, normalized URL, nonce, time, and optional access-token hash; shared verification checks the public-only JWK and bindings, while the server atomically consumes a thumbprint-plus-jti replay key.
preconditions: persistent key store where stable identity is required
traps: without IndexedDB the browser key is not persistent
query and fragment are excluded while scheme, host, port, and path remain binding-sensitive
replay safety is fleet-wide only with shared atomic storage
the selected corpus does not prove token issuance policy or replay-record retention
portable_playbook: create one persistent installation key
bind each proof to method and trusted normalized URL
include the token hash on protected requests
strictly validate JWK, time, signature, and bindings
atomically consume thumbprint plus nonce with explicit retention

retained_failures_and_limits: No T3 source tests were executed in this study; test files are inspected evidence only.

Static source evidence does not prove crash consistency, cross-provider semantic equivalence, ACP interoperability with every peer, OS-level concurrency, cryptographic correctness, or deployment-wide replay storage.
Only paths content-bound by the manifest selected corpus are cited.
This packet contains no OpenAgents target code, target fit, implementation state, acceptance, release, or public capability verdict.
